name: Cyclic OSKM ameliorated age-associated hallmarks in mice
summary: Ocampo 2016 is the core animal-signal anchor for Partial Reprogramming: cyclic OSKM improved age-associated cellular and physiological phenotypes, prolonged lifespan in a premature-aging mouse model, and improved recovery in older wild-type mice.
statement: Short-term cyclic OSKM expression ameliorated cellular and physiological hallmarks of aging and prolonged lifespan in a premature-aging mouse model, while also improving recovery from metabolic disease and muscle injury in older wild-type mice.
population_or_model: Premature-aging mouse model and older wild-type mice
time_horizon: Short-term cyclic OSKM induction with lifespan and recovery follow-up in mouse models
quantitative_note: PubMed abstract reports lifespan prolongation in the premature-aging mouse model but does not provide the exact effect size.
caveats: The lifespan result is in a premature-aging model, not normal human aging.
Older wild-type mouse evidence is functional recovery context rather than human intervention evidence.
OSKM includes c-Myc, so schedule and safety control are central to interpretation.
